title: EnvelopeRevocation
description: Body of POST /v1/envelopes/revoke — the per-answer override that retracts ONE of the user's own previously-submitted envelopes (ARCHITECTURE.md §1.12 'override is sacred'). The broker recovers the user's identity from delegation_token (the only thing that identifies them) and the target answer from (question_id, delegation_token.unique_identifier), then deletes that envelope and rebuilds the question's aggregate from the remaining envelopes in the same transaction. Idempotent: revoking an already-revoked or never-submitted answer returns accepted=true. The end-to-end test asserts the body contains exactly these three fields and no others (boundary-leakage check, §12) — there is no answer text, no question text, no rationale, no demographic data on the revocation wire.

revocation_signature.description: base64 Ed25519 signature (64 bytes) over H('REVOKE' | question_id | delegation_hash), signed by the agent_key bound in delegation_token. The literal 'REVOKE' prefix is the domain separator that prevents a captured envelope signature (which signs H(question_id || answer || nonce || delegation_hash)) from being replayed as a revocation.
